blockingIssues.description: The new browser-resolution test suite never exercises the existing `env.BROWSER.launch()` branch in `resolveBrowser()`, so VAL-BWIRE-004 is not fully satisfied and that path could regress unnoticed.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that the production Cloudflare Browser Rendering binding is the fetch-based `env.BROWSER` object passed directly to `puppeteer.launch(env.BROWSER)`, while browser resolution also supports injected `BrowserLike` doubles and bindings exposing `launch()`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align browser-executor snapshot guidance with the runtime contract by describing interactive elements with `role`, `label`, and `selector` fields instead of `type`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `puppeteer-browser-adapter` review found the mission feature spec and `cf-worker` skill still describing snapshot interactive elements as having `type` fields, while the actual runtime contract uses `ObservationInteractiveElement.role` in `apps/browser-executor/src/observationBuilder.ts` and `BrowserPageSnapshot`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document how workers should handle unrelated pre-existing dirty shared-state files during cleanup and commit preparation so feature handoffs are not blocked by ambient `.factory` changes they did not introduce.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `puppeteer-browser-adapter` review found the worker had to deviate from the expected cleanup flow because unrelated `.factory/library/*` files were already dirty, but no project skill documented the correct procedure for that scenario.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax `cf-worker` guidance for deployment-only Cloudflare tasks so it explicitly allows necessary `wrangler.toml` compatibility updates and remote `wrangler deploy` plus public health-check verification when local `wrangler dev` is not the relevant validation surface.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `deploy-browser-executor` review found the feature needed a `nodejs_compat` change in `apps/browser-executor/wrangler.toml` and validation against the deployed workers.dev endpoint, which conflicts with current `cf-worker` instructions that require local `wrangler dev` verification and say not to modify `wrangler.toml`.
